title: Purged-embargoed CPCV for blend and feature selection, scored by worst-path not mean AUC
summary: Public time-series validation references emphasize purging and embargo when labels overlap temporally, and CPCV gives a distribution of out-of-sample paths instead of one average score.
rationale: Public time-series validation references emphasize purging and embargo when labels overlap temporally, and CPCV gives a distribution of out-of-sample paths instead of one average score. Earlier research already suspected optimistic CV; the actionable extension is to use path-robust selection for ensemble weights and risky features.
implementation_hint: Split chronologically by event blocks, purge rows near test boundaries by at least the short pit horizon, add an embargo buffer, and generate several CPCV-style paths. Re-score every candidate model/blend by CVaR or mean-minus-std across paths, then choose ensemble weights and optional feature subsets by that robust score instead of plain mean ROC AUC.
expected_effect: May lower local CV slightly but should reduce leaderboard fragility and help decide between many near-tied blend variants around 0.936.
risk: Less training data per fold and more compute. If the purge window is too aggressive, variance can swamp the signal.
